# InfraBench

> InfraBench evaluates AI agents on realistic infrastructure incidents across the full stack and operational lifecycle — durability, invariants, cleanup, and risk — not just binary pass/fail.

## Docs

- [Project website](https://infraben.ch/): Overview, task heatmap, failure modes, and taxonomy
- [Leaderboard](https://infraben.ch/leaderboard): Full agent/model rankings
- [Documentation](https://infraben.ch/docs): Introduction, contribute, and submit-a-task guides
- [Introduction](https://infraben.ch/docs/introduction): What InfraBench evaluates
- [Contribute](https://infraben.ch/docs/contribute): How to contribute task packages
- [Submit a task](https://infraben.ch/docs/submit-task): Task layout and PR checklist
- [Paper (PDF)](https://hotinfra.org/2026/papers/hotinfra26-final71.pdf)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/XuanmiaoG/InfraBench)
- [Hugging Face org](https://huggingface.co/InfraBench)

## Optional

- [Medium essay](https://medium.com/@gaoy31/your-ai-agent-passed-swe-bench-it-would-still-take-down-production-f1235315f112): Your AI Agent Passed SWE-bench. It Would Still Take Down Production.
- [HotInfra 2026](https://hotinfra.org/2026/): Workshop page (co-located with ISCA '26)
- [Google Scholar (Yuan Gao)](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=oD9j2NMAAAAJ&hl=en): Lead author publication profile
